bash_commands.command: find /workspace/kubernetes--v1.32.0 -name "*.go" -type f | xargs grep -l "SubjectAccessReview" | grep -E "(registry|handler|rest)" | head -20

  "text": "The Kubernetes RBAC authorization implementation is a comprehensive role-based access control system distributed across multiple critical components:\n\n## Core Authorization Engine\n\nThe **RBACAuthorizer** (plugin/pkg/auth/authorizer/rbac/rbac.go) is the main authorization engine implementing the Authorize method. It uses a RequestToRuleMapper interface to evaluate authorization decisions by checking policy rules against request attributes. The RBACAuthorizer has methods for both authorization (Authorize) and retrieving user rules (RulesFor).\n\n## Rule Resolution and Subject Evaluation\n\nThe **DefaultRuleResolver** (pkg/registry/rbac/validation/rule.go) resolves PolicyRules for a user in a given namespace by:\n- Implementing RulesFor() which returns all applicable rules\n- Implementing VisitRulesFor() for iterative rule evaluation\n- Implementing GetRoleReferenceRules() to resolve role references in bindings\n- Supporting both cluster-wide and namespace-scoped bindings\n\nThe **SubjectAccessEvaluator** (plugin/pkg/auth/authorizer/rbac/subject_locator.go) identifies which subjects (users, groups, service accounts) can perform an action by examining all role bindings and evaluating their rules against request attributes.\n\n## Subject Access Review Handlers\n\nMultiple REST handlers process authorization review requests:\n- **SubjectAccessReview** (pkg/registry/authorization/subjectaccessreview/rest.go) - cluster-wide authorization review\n- **LocalSubjectAccessReview** (pkg/registry/authorization/localsubjectaccessreview/rest.go) - namespace-scoped review\n- **SelfSubjectAccessReview** (pkg/registry/authorization/selfsubjectaccessreview/rest.go) - self-authorization queries\n- **SelfSubjectRulesReview** (pkg/registry/authorization/selfsubjectrulesreview/rest.go) - enumerates allowed rules for current user\n\nAll handlers use the Authorizer interface to evaluate decisions and return detailed status information.\n\n## Bootstrap RBAC Policies\n\nThree bootstrap policy files define initial RBAC configuration:\n- **policy.go** (plugin/pkg/auth/authorizer/rbac/bootstrappolicy/policy.go) exports ClusterRoles() and ClusterRoleBindings() containing system-wide roles\n- **controller_policy.go** (plugin/pkg/auth/authorizer/rbac/bootstrappolicy/controller_policy.go) exports ControllerRoles() and ControllerRoleBindings() for Kubernetes controllers\n- **namespace_policy.go** (plugin/pkg/auth/authorizer/rbac/bootstrappolicy/namespace_policy.go) exports NamespaceRoles() and NamespaceRoleBindings() for per-namespace defaults\n\nThese are applied during API server startup via the PostStartHook mechanism in storage_rbac.go.\n\n## Policy-Based Storage with Escalation Prevention\n\nThe policy-based storage implementations prevent privilege escalation:\n- **Role storage** (pkg/registry/rbac/role/policybased/storage.go) - namespace-scoped roles\n- **RoleBinding storage** (pkg/registry/rbac/rolebinding/policybased/storage.go) - namespace-scoped bindings\n- **ClusterRole storage** (pkg/registry/rbac/clusterrole/policybased/storage.go) - cluster-wide roles with aggregation support\n- **ClusterRoleBinding storage** (pkg/registry/rbac/clusterrolebinding/policybased/storage.go) - cluster-wide bindings\n\nAll storage implementations:\n- Call EscalationAllowed() (pkg/registry/rbac/escalation_check.go) to allow system:masters group\n- Call RoleEscalationAuthorized() to check explicit escalate permission\n- Call ConfirmNoEscalation/ConfirmNoEscalationInternal to ensure users cannot grant permissions they don't have\n- Wrap updates with validation logic to prevent privilege escalation during Create/Update operations\n\n## Supporting Infrastructure\n\n- **escalation_check.go** provides helper functions for checking escalation authorization\n- **validation/rule.go** implements core rule matching and validation logic including ConfirmNoEscalation\n- **validation/internal_version_adapter.go** bridges internal and v1 API versions for escalation checking\n- **helpers.go** provides utility functions for object manipulation and GC field detection\n- **authorization/util/helpers.go** converts API objects to authorizer.AttributesRecord for evaluation\n- **storage_rbac.go** coordinates all RBAC storage setup and bootstrap policy installation\n- **storage_authorization.go** coordinates all authorization review handler registration"
}

conversation_events.text:
# Kubernetes RBAC Authorization Implementation

## Your Task

Find all Go source files in kubernetes/kubernetes that implement RBAC authorization. Identify: the RBACAuthorizer with its Authorize method (in plugin/pkg/auth/authorizer/rbac/), the SubjectAccessEvaluator, the DefaultRuleResolver that resolves PolicyRules for a user, the SubjectAccessReview REST handler, the bootstrap RBAC policy definitions, and the policy-based storage for Role and ClusterRoleBinding objects with escalation prevention.

## Context

You are working on a codebase task involving repos from the security domain.

## Available Resources

The local `/workspace/` directory contains: sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.32.0, sg-evals/client-go--v0.32.0, sg-evals/api--v0.32.0, sg-evals/etcd-io-etcd.

**Note:** Additional repositories are accessible via Sourcegraph MCP tools:
- `sg-evals/etcd-io-etcd` (etcd-io/etcd)

## Output Format

Create a file at `/workspace/answer.json` with your findings in the following structure:

```json
{
  "files": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go"}
  ],
  "symbols": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go", "symbol": "SymbolName"}
  ],
  "chain": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go", "symbol": "FunctionName"}
  ],
  "text": "Narrative explanation of your findings, citing repos and file paths."
}
```

Include only the fields relevant to this task. Your answer is evaluated against a closed-world oracle — completeness matters.

## Evaluation

Your answer will be scored on:
- **File recall and precision**: Did you find all relevant files?
I'll help you find all Go source files related to Kubernetes RBAC authorization implementation. Let me start by exploring the workspace structure and the kubernetes repository.
Let me explore the RBAC authorization directory in the kubernetes repository:
Now let me explore the RBAC registry validation directory where DefaultRuleResolver is defined:
Now let me search for SubjectAccessReview handler:
Now let me compile all the findings into the required JSON format. Let me create the answer file:

Perfect! I've completed a comprehensive analysis of the Kubernetes RBAC authorization implementation. Here's what I found:

## Summary

I identified **21 key Go source files** across kubernetes/kubernetes v1.32.0 that implement RBAC authorization, including:

### Core Components:
1. **RBACAuthorizer** (`plugin/pkg/auth/authorizer/rbac/rbac.go`) - The main authorization engine with the Authorize method
2. **SubjectAccessEvaluator** (`plugin/pkg/auth/authorizer/rbac/subject_locator.go`) - Identifies allowed subjects
3. **DefaultRuleResolver** (`pkg/registry/rbac/validation/rule.go`) - Resolves PolicyRules for users

### SubjectAccessReview Handlers:
- SubjectAccessReview (cluster-wide)
- LocalSubjectAccessReview (namespace-scoped)
- SelfSubjectAccessReview (self queries)
- SelfSubjectRulesReview (rule enumeration)

### Bootstrap Policies:
- `bootstrappolicy/policy.go` - Cluster roles and bindings
- `bootstrappolicy/controller_policy.go` - Controller roles
- `bootstrappolicy/namespace_policy.go` - Namespace defaults

### Policy-Based Storage with Escalation Prevention:
- Role, RoleBinding, ClusterRole, and ClusterRoleBinding storage implementations
- Escalation check functions (`escalation_check.go`)
- Confirmation mechanisms (`ConfirmNoEscalation`, `ConfirmNoEscalationInternal`)

The JSON file at `/workspace/answer.json` contains complete details with 47 key symbols and 22 important functions in the call chain, along with a comprehensive narrative explanation.

  ],
  "text": "The Kubernetes RBAC authorization implementation is a comprehensive role-based access control system distributed across multiple critical components:\n\n## Core Authorization Engine\n\nThe **RBACAuthorizer** (plugin/pkg/auth/authorizer/rbac/rbac.go) is the main authorization engine implementing the Authorize method. It uses a RequestToRuleMapper interface to evaluate authorization decisions by checking policy rules against request attributes. The RBACAuthorizer has methods for both authorization (Authorize) and retrieving user rules (RulesFor).\n\n## Rule Resolution and Subject Evaluation\n\nThe **DefaultRuleResolver** (pkg/registry/rbac/validation/rule.go) resolves PolicyRules for a user in a given namespace by:\n- Implementing RulesFor() which returns all applicable rules\n- Implementing VisitRulesFor() for iterative rule evaluation\n- Implementing GetRoleReferenceRules() to resolve role references in bindings\n- Supporting both cluster-wide and namespace-scoped bindings\n\nThe **SubjectAccessEvaluator** (plugin/pkg/auth/authorizer/rbac/subject_locator.go) identifies which subjects (users, groups, service accounts) can perform an action by examining all role bindings and evaluating their rules against request attributes.\n\n## Subject Access Review Handlers\n\nMultiple REST handlers process authorization review requests:\n- **SubjectAccessReview** (pkg/registry/authorization/subjectaccessreview/rest.go) - cluster-wide authorization review\n- **LocalSubjectAccessReview** (pkg/registry/authorization/localsubjectaccessreview/rest.go) - namespace-scoped review\n- **SelfSubjectAccessReview** (pkg/registry/authorization/selfsubjectaccessreview/rest.go) - self-authorization queries\n- **SelfSubjectRulesReview** (pkg/registry/authorization/selfsubjectrulesreview/rest.go) - enumerates allowed rules for current user\n\nAll handlers use the Authorizer interface to evaluate decisions and return detailed status information.\n\n## Bootstrap RBAC Policies\n\nThree bootstrap policy files define initial RBAC configuration:\n- **policy.go** (plugin/pkg/auth/authorizer/rbac/bootstrappolicy/policy.go) exports ClusterRoles() and ClusterRoleBindings() containing system-wide roles\n- **controller_policy.go** (plugin/pkg/auth/authorizer/rbac/bootstrappolicy/controller_policy.go) exports ControllerRoles() and ControllerRoleBindings() for Kubernetes controllers\n- **namespace_policy.go** (plugin/pkg/auth/authorizer/rbac/bootstrappolicy/namespace_policy.go) exports NamespaceRoles() and NamespaceRoleBindings() for per-namespace defaults\n\nThese are applied during API server startup via the PostStartHook mechanism in storage_rbac.go.\n\n## Policy-Based Storage with Escalation Prevention\n\nThe policy-based storage implementations prevent privilege escalation:\n- **Role storage** (pkg/registry/rbac/role/policybased/storage.go) - namespace-scoped roles\n- **RoleBinding storage** (pkg/registry/rbac/rolebinding/policybased/storage.go) - namespace-scoped bindings\n- **ClusterRole storage** (pkg/registry/rbac/clusterrole/policybased/storage.go) - cluster-wide roles with aggregation support\n- **ClusterRoleBinding storage** (pkg/registry/rbac/clusterrolebinding/policybased/storage.go) - cluster-wide bindings\n\nAll storage implementations:\n- Call EscalationAllowed() (pkg/registry/rbac/escalation_check.go) to allow system:masters group\n- Call RoleEscalationAuthorized() to check explicit escalate permission\n- Call ConfirmNoEscalation/ConfirmNoEscalationInternal to ensure users cannot grant permissions they don't have\n- Wrap updates with validation logic to prevent privilege escalation during Create/Update operations\n\n## Supporting Infrastructure\n\n- **escalation_check.go** provides helper functions for checking escalation authorization\n- **validation/rule.go** implements core rule matching and validation logic including ConfirmNoEscalation\n- **validation/internal_version_adapter.go** bridges internal and v1 API versions for escalation checking\n- **helpers.go** provides utility functions for object manipulation and GC field detection\n- **authorization/util/helpers.go** converts API objects to authorizer.AttributesRecord for evaluation\n- **storage_rbac.go** coordinates all RBAC storage setup and bootstrap policy installation\n- **storage_authorization.go** coordinates all authorization review handler registration"
}
